svg.title: Production RAG System Architecture
svg.desc: Full architecture diagram of a production-grade RAG system with FastAPI, Celery, Qdrant, PostgreSQL, Redis, and monitoring stack
svg.text: FastAPI · LangGraph · Qdrant · Celery · Hybrid Retrieval · Full Observability

g.text: REST endpoints · JWT Bearer auth · OAuth2 · Rate limited (slowapi + Redis) · CORS
Gunicorn · 4 × UvicornWorker · Python 3.11 · Slim Docker image (~800 MB)
Query rewrite → dense retrieve → sparse (BM25) → RRF merge → rerank → generate
AsyncPostgresSaver checkpointer · per-thread conversation memory · GPT-4o-mini
Broker · BM25 cache · token blocklist · job state · rate limit · session
PyPDF parse (parallel threads) → dedup (SHA-256) → embed (text-embedding-3-small, batch 64)
→ semantic merge (cosine ≥ 0.88) → upsert Qdrant (batch 256) → BM25 rebuild → Redis persist
Chunk: 1000 tok / 250 overlap · cap 2000 chunks/doc · max 50 MB upload
Dense: Qdrant similarity top-30 · Sparse: BM25Okapi top-30 · RRF merge · Cross-encoder rerank top-8
BAAI/bge-reranker-base (worker) · FlashRank fallback (API) · context window 80k chars
11 containers · named volumes · 127.0.0.1 bindings · health checks · restart: unless-stopped
Why this is different from a standard RAG
✦ Hybrid retrieval: dense + sparse + rerank (vs. dense-only)
✦ Persistent conversation memory via PostgreSQL (vs. in-memory / stateless)
✦ Async Celery pipeline: API never blocks on ingest (vs. background task in-process)
✦ Full observability: Prometheus + Loki + Grafana + LangSmith tracing
